entry.title: Re: 2024-07 Developer Challenge - Task 0 - Server and service provisioning
entry.content:
Nicely tested though anyway 😉

Actually, this allows me to point out to folks the importance of what the `serviceurl` is.

In your screenshot you have:

`https://ajmadariaga-simplest-deployment.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/odata/v4/catalog/Books`

... but this is NOT a service URL. It's the URL of a specific entity set

`/Books`

_within_ the service

`/odata/v4/catalog`

So the value for the serviceurl here should have been:

`https://ajmadariaga-simplest-deployment.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/odata/v4/catalog`

Please note the difference, folks! Services can have multiple endpoints, e.g. `/Books`, `/Authors`, etc, each with their own specific URLs.

Thank you for providing me with an opportunity to make this distinction clear!

entry.title: A Question regarding Editable ALV Report.
entry.content:
I have created a CDS view from a Z table. Then I am trying to consume the CDS view in an ALV report where I want "MENGE" field(Purchase order quantity) to be editable. That is I am trying to create an editable ALV report. But I am receiving some error, Hence I am unable to do so. Please help me to find the solution.  Following is the program : 

DATA: gt_data TYPE TABLE OF ZCDS_PO_02,
      gr_alv  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
      gr_table TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
       gr_columns TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns_table,
      gr_column TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  SELECT * FROM ZCDS_PO_02 INTO TABLE gt_data.

  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table = gr_alv
        CHANGING
          t_table      = gt_data
      ).

" Get column settings for MENGE field
      gr_column = gr_alv->get_columns( )->get_column( 'MENGE' ).

      IF gr_column IS BOUND.
        " Set MENGE field editable
        gr_column->set_cell_editable( abap_true ).
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE 'Column MENGE not found' TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.

    CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO DATA(lx_salv_msg).
      MESSAGE lx_salv_msg TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

Error :

Please help me to remove the error and you may also share alternative approach if any.

entry.title: Re: EWM - Delete Handling Unit when adding to existing Ha...
entry.content:
...looked at this topic also. I think these are the options that you have:

- putaway WT is a product WT: change destination HU via exception code CHHU (source HU will be deleted automatically)

- putaway WT is a HU WT: change destination HU via exception code CHHU and unpack the source HU via an implementation of Badi /scwm/ex_core_co_unp_outhu 

How to control the creation of product vs. HU WT was already mentioned in other comments below I think - won't repeat that here. 

Hope that helps!

Regards,

Hendrik

entry.title: Re: Where can I remove a learning recommendation that I m...
entry.content:
Hi @Jake_Berger, 

I did some research due to the new ILX, the recommendation engine and the Newsletter couple of months ago. Recommendations cannot be removed easily from the Learner. This also varies by the method of the recommendation. The best control is with the user receiving the recommendation. Meaning, he is the one in charge of the recommendations he received. They will only be listed under the recommendation section, so no direct assignment showing him up. 

As Admin, I think the only option you have is to use proxy feature and remove the recommendation manually. 
I thought the ASP would be easy, as you can also unassign the assigned items, but this does not work for recommendation 😞

I hope this helps. 

Cheers, 
Marc

entry.title: Re: BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits
entry.content:
Hi Jason,

For technical questions about the application it would be easier to open an 'issue' on github, so we can discuss it there. But to resolve your 'snag' I suggest the following:

  • Ensure that the Work Zone subscription is active in the same sub account as where you deployed the CF application.
  • It is the CF application deployment process that will create the 'btprc-srv' destination in the sub account for you. If this seems to be missing, you can try to redo the deployment via the 'cf deploy' command mentioned in the documentation.

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q2 ’24)
entry.content:
As a continuation of the previous blog, we would like to provide further updates and keep up the momentum of sharing relevant information with our community.

In this blog series, learn about the new content packages published on SAP Business Accelerator Hub from our partners during Q2 '2024, along with their high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, refer to the partners listing which is available on the home page of SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

_____________________________________________________________________________________

Kaar Technologies

Integration package published:

KTern.AI Digital Maps Integration with SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA

This package contains reference information about KTern.AI Digital Maps integration with SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA, SAP Business Suite to assess and plan SAP led Digital Transformations. KTern.AI is an SAP recognized Spotlight App on SAP Store and Industry Cloud solution to help enterprises globally with data-driven automation and Generative AI led business transformation

SAP-validated partner use case (VPUC) published:

KTern.AI for RISE with SAP DXaaS

Pioneer AI-First Transformations with Clean Core Strategy using KTern.AI driven ‘Digital Transformation as a Service’ (DXaaS) delivery automation for RISE with SAP.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PiLog India Pvt Ltd

API package published:

PiLog Preferred Records

The integration of PiLog Preferred Records (PPR) with SAP S/4HANA Cloud brings Standardized Material and Service Master Records into your organization. PiLog Preferred Record Structured material description contains the Genuine Manufacturer, Genuine Part Number/Model Number and Standardized characteristic values.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Coveo Solutions Inc

API package published:

Coveo Commerce

Coveo for Commerce drives incremental revenue for B2B and B2C organizations through AI-powered product discovery. With extensible APIs for your SAP Commerce Cloud storefront you can create relevant search, optimized product listing pages and personalized product recommendations.

Coveo Search Token

Coveo offers personalized search and recommendations across product and content, customer specific price lists, relevance generative answering, and more. The Coveo Search Token Omni Commerce Connect (OCC) Web Service powers your storefront with a secure and efficient way to access the Coveo Commerce and Search APIs. This endpoint integrates with SAP Commerce Cloud by allowing it to retrieve the Coveo search token for authentication and make it available to storefronts.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Qintesi S.p.A

API package published:

Namirial eSignAnyWhere

Discover smoother operations and enhanced security with our signature integration for SAP! Our solution makes signing documents easy and stylish. Simplify your work and increase productivity while ensuring your signatures are secure and legally valid. Immerse yourself in a world of efficiency and accuracy with our SAP signature integration.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crave InfoTech

SAP-validated partner use case (VPUC) published:

Account Payable Automation – Gate to Pay

Customer's Account Payable (AP) processes faced challenges in terms of data inflow points, manual handling of thousands of invoices daily, delays in the Material Invoice Receipt Order (MIRO) parking, and the need for a streamlined approach to invoice data publishing for settlements.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TARENTO

SAP-validated partner use case (VPUC) published:

iVolve - A Tarento Framework to Empower Transformation

Strategy, selection, migration & consolidation of Enterprise Integration Platform as a Service (EiPaaS).

  • iVolve’s Integration Platform Recommendation Tool - Gain rapid insight into potential marketplace integration platforms that solves your as-is/to-be integration vision, styles & use cases.
  • iVolve’s Vector Sprint - A fixed priced, time bound discovery sprint evaluating a target platform against integration vision, use cases, styles, patterns, domains & modernization demands. Detailed outcome driven workshops drive insight & artifacts for implementation.
  • iVolve Migration Automation Framework - Automated & Accelerated integration re-platforming to the cloud. Leverage accelerators & assets on the framework to engage your transformation with guidance & delivery from our integration competence center.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Wipro Limited

SAP-validated partner use case (VPUC) published:

Smart Energy Procurement Advisor

Natural Language Query (NLQ) enabled assist platform for Energy Procurement executive, bound by standard procurement policy guidelines. Utilizes Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) model abstracted via Langchain framework, powered by AOAI (GPT Turbo 3.5 / text-ada-002 models via Gen AI hub). Employs multi-dimensional spatial distance ranking to present historical top results. Simulate custom scenarios, override base policies, and trigger workflows for stakeholder approvals. Full track and trace features. Dashboard to manage events and maintain closure details and future adoption to Joule.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KPMG S.p.A

SAP-validated partner use cases (VPUC) published:

Innovative Real-Time Forecasting Process

This project harnesses the real-time computing capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud to seamlessly integrate and standardize data from diverse sources, including both cloud and non-cloud systems, as well as SAP and non-SAP environments. Leveraging SAP Analytics Cloud, an integrated model has been created to facilitate financial and managerial planning. Through automated processes called data actions, essential calculations are performed efficiently. The final step involves presenting the processed data on tailor-made dashboards, providing a comprehensive and insightful view for informed decision-making.

Intelligent Forecasting with SAP AI Core

This project seeks to implement artificial intelligence logic into the planning process, aiming to generate practical solutions. Upon receiving real-time and historical data inputs, the applied AI techniques will compute forecasting and budgetary information. The culminating data will then be seamlessly incorporated into SAP Analytics Cloud for visualization and potential adjustments.

Manage Corporate Income Taxes

The use case encompasses the evaluation of Corporate Income Taxes, including IRES and IRAP for Italian markets, and others based on individual country requirements, along with Deferred Tax Assets and Deferred Tax Liabilities in alignment with local and IFRS Gaap. The system utilizes SAP Analytics Cloud capabilities to automatically gather data from source systems, including GL, HR, and assets, with the option to upload necessary data through an Excel file. Employing a pre-designed form, the tax department can manually adjust data at various stages of the process, ensuring complete and accurate traceability during data collection and after variation impact calculation. Automatic rules facilitate the association of GL Accounts with Tax Variations and the assessment of their impact on corporate income tax. Additionally, dashboards are provided for the analysis of Tax Data and reconciliation of Tax Rates.

Manage Strategic Planning

Helps the end customer to fully manage both the strategic planning and process consolidation, ensuring maximum transparency and continuous monitoring of the entire process progress.

KSelf - SuccessFactors Self Service Dashboard By KPMG

The main objective of the custom solution is to make employees completely autonomous in modifying their data through a secure tool that applies all the necessary checks before saving the data in the Employee Central module.

Manage Regional Ceilings for Training Dashboard

Helps in effective management of item/class for all areas, Automated centralized management of seats to be assigned to each area, Integration with Learning Management System and SAP SuccessFactors for integrated reporting.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Twenty5 LLC

Integration package published:

Twenty5 Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

This integration package contains standard integration flows to synchronize the commercial project between Twenty5 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud by leveraging the ODATA services provided by SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RealCore Group GmbH

Integration package published:

SAP LeanIX Integration with SAP Cloud Integration

This integration package enables data synchronization between SAP Cloud Integration Content and SAP LeanIX factsheets by reading specific SAP Cloud Integration objects and transferring them to SAP LeanIX application.

SAP LeanIX Integration with SAP Process Orchestration

This integration package enables data synchronization between SAP Process Orchestration Content and SAP LeanIX factsheets by reading specific SAP Process Orchestration objects and transferring them to SAP LeanIX application.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section and discover center mission for more details. 

Stay tuned for further updates and information by the end of the next quarter!

entry.title: Re: S4HC Warehouse Inventory Report for a single item rec...
entry.content:
Hello @ksnv 

You can't pack a single piece of a product into three different handling units nor can you place it into different bins. That is possible neither physically nor in the system. 

In fact, you don't have a single product but a set composed of three components i.e. doors, glass and frame. They should be managed with individual material numbers as you want to place them separately. From the stock management, warehouse operations and logistics perspective, these are three individual materials - they are packed separately, placed into different bins etc. 

Having the components managed under separate material numbers would still allow them to be sold as a complete set with Bills of Materials in Sales Documents functionality.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Decoding SAP BTP: Simplifying Business Technology
entry.content:
As a marketing professional, I've noticed that many new customers are curious about SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) and its architecture. Drawing from a few customer engagements and insights instances, here's a simple and holistic overview of SAP BTP, its evolution, key components, and how it can benefit your business.

The Evolution of SAP BTP

SAP BTP has gone through several name changes and iterations. Initially called SAP HANA Cloud Platform, it was later renamed SAP Cloud Platform (SCP). The goal was to offer a versatile platform integrating various technologies, not just SAP HANA, but also open-source technologies like Java and MongoDB. Eventually, the name was changed to SAP Business Technology Platform to reflect its focus on business requirements and a wide range of technologies.

The Role of SAP BTP in Modern Business

At its core, SAP BTP aims to streamline the usage of SAP's extensive services. Imagine trying to use over 90 standalone services independently. Each service would have its own setup, creating a fragmented experience for users. SAP BTP addresses this by unifying these services, making it easier for businesses to manage and deploy them.

Key Components of SAP BTP

Identity Authentication Service (IAS):

IAS is crucial for managing user access to services on BTP. It ensures that only authorized users can access specific services, providing a seamless login experience across SAP cloud products.

Connectivity & Destination:

This component acts as a proxy, facilitating the interconnection of various services. It simplifies the process of connecting SAP BTP with external systems, ensuring secure and efficient data communication.

Customer Subaccount and Space:

Subaccounts and spaces help organize services and resources within BTP. Subaccounts provide a broader organizational structure, while spaces allow for finer granularity, helping teams manage their specific tasks efficiently.

SAP Cloud Connector:

Although not part of BTP itself, the SAP Cloud Connector is essential for connecting on-premise systems with SAP BTP. It acts as a reverse proxy, ensuring secure communication between your local systems and the cloud.

BTP Runtime:

The runtime environment, particularly Cloud Foundry, is where applications and services are developed and deployed. It provides a container for these services, supporting various programming languages and frameworks.

Why Choose SAP BTP?

Without SAP BTP, managing SAP's diverse services would be a daunting task. BTP simplifies this by providing a unified platform, enhancing efficiency and user experience. It integrates various technologies and services, making it easier for businesses to innovate and scale.

Getting Started with SAP BTP

For those new to SAP BTP, starting with hands-on tutorials can be incredibly helpful. SAP offers a range of resources to get you acquainted with the platform. Dive into a development tutorial and explore the capabilities of BTP.

SAP BTP is a powerful tool that simplifies the complexity of managing SAP's extensive suite of services. Whether you're looking to streamline operations, enhance data management, or develop new applications, SAP BTP provides a robust and versatile platform to meet your business needs.

entry.title: To-Many Relationship using CDS , SAP CAP
entry.content:
Hi,


I have created a To-Many Association in CDS.

using {cuid} from '@sap/cds/common';

service MyService {
    entity Books : cuid {
        title  : String;
        descr  : String;
        genre  : Genre;
        author : Association to Authors;
    }

    entity Authors : cuid {
        name  : String;
        books : Association to many Books
                    on books.author = $self;
    }

    type Genre : String enum {
        Mystery;
        Fiction;
        Drama;
    }
}

 

On POST Request to : http://localhost:8080/odata/v4/MyService/Authors
Request Body: 

{
    "name": "Jane Doe",
    "books": [
        {
            "title": "TechEd",
            "descr": "TechEd related topics",
            "genre": "Mystery"
        },
        {
            "title": "TechEd",
            "descr": "TechEd related topics",
            "genre": "Mystery"
        }
    ]
}

 We are getting two different responses on seperate systems.


First Response:

{
    "error": {
        "code": "500",
        "message": "Cannot set reference MyService.Authors.books. Reverse associations are not supported."
    }
}

Second Response:

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Authors(books())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"e81376e1bf5355c00f19bd5e515a73e2fec21676ca685de6f37c5220ed5ccf9b\"",
    "ID": "61c3ac63-0886-46e3-a700-b1787c4d2f6b",
    "name": "Jane Doe",
    "books": [
        {
            "ID": "f0b0e303-fdfc-4d5b-b8a3-5d97dcf63b3b",
            "title": "TechEd",
            "descr": "TechEd related topics",
            "genre": "Mystery",
            "author_ID": null
        },
        {
            "ID": "0b4192c6-ea1d-409d-ba97-5ce6ed90ec56",
            "title": "TechEd",
            "descr": "TechEd related topics",
            "genre": "Mystery",
            "author_ID": null
        }
    ]
}

 How is this possible?
Please let me know.

Thanks.

entry.content:

entry.title: 🇳🇱 Hands-on with the btp CLI and APIs
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the Core Service APIs for SAP BTP and also the command line interface tool for SAP BTP, otherwise known as the btp CLI.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to btp, the CLI for the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). It also introduces attendees to the APIs for SAP BTP, known as Core Services for SAP BTP, which, according to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, allow you to "manage, build, and extend the core capabilities of SAP BTP."

After completing this CodeJam, you have gained an overview of some of the core components from which SAP BTP is composed. You will also be then familiar with how to use the btp CLI on the command line, and also in scripts, to view, administer and generally manage these components and resources on SAP BTP. You'll also have a good understanding of what is possible with the APIs, and perhaps more critically, how to prepare and use authentication for them.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wed 25 Sep 2024 in Utrecht, NL. The language of the event and the content will be English.

The event is kindly being hosted by Capgemini Nederland, thank you! The location of the event is their offices (see event location detail map), specifically Room N.3.15.5.

Please aim to arrive at 0900 local time to get set up and plugged in, for a start at 0930 local time. We should be finished by around 1600 local time.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-btp-cli-api-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md 

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. In that context, if you indicate that you're attending, and later realise you're unable to, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Customizing the Sales Data file upload and challenges faced
entry.content:
Sales Data is an important parameter which is needed to be imported in Emrarsys. Sales data adds up to the revenue metrics as well as data for Smart Insight. This data needs to be configured in a certain order to be imported into Emarsys. In this blog we will discuss the configuration of sales data.

Sales data can be imported into Emarsys via 2 methods, either via Smart Insight SFTP or Emarsys Predict API. It is preferred to use Predict API as it provides a fast as well as immediate verification result which can result into faster troubleshooting. We will be discussing the latter in detail here. The file is configured in a CSV format and consist of header with standard as well as custom fields. Each line consists of a single line item of a particular order. For example, if an order consists of 6 different products, then that order will consist of 6 lines with each product with its quantity in one line. This file should be named sales_items*.csv where * can be any random sets of character. This file needs to be a CSV file with UTF-8 coding.

 

Fields in Emarsys

Standard Fields

Types of fields in Sales data file are of 2 types, Standard and custom. Standard fields are required field and these fields needs to be in it’s particular data type as well. Thers are standard fields in Emarsys

 

Field

Data Type

Description

Example

item

String

The unique Product ID which is purchased

ABC01

price

Float

Price of the product

25.36

order

String

Order Number

XY3456-T

timestamp

Timestamp in UTC

Time of transaction

2021-12-6T15:02:00Z

customer

String

Customer number

001234432

Email

String(email)

Email Id of the customer

Exp@test.com

quantity

Float

Quantity purchased

2.0

     

Here both, customer and email can not be configured in the file. Only one can be used for your sales data. This is discussed in detail below.

Custom Fields

Custom Fields are an important aspect of Emarsys Configuration file as additional information such as local currency. These custom fields are added as a new column in the file. Naming convention for these fields are also pre-defined by Emarsys which is DataTypePrefix_Name. The available datatypes available are as follow

Datatype

Prefix

Integer

i_*

Float

f_*

Timestamp

t_*

String

s_*

 

Challenges in Preparing the file -:

Converting multiple sales data order in Emarsys Format(order per line format)
Sales Data in Emarsys needs to be in The Emarsys format which is line per order and as it is not possible to get sales Data in the Emarsys format by Design. Here we are taking SAP ECC system as our source system.
To get sales data in the expected format we have used SAP CPI.

 


As data coming from ECC contains multiple orders in one line a splitter is used to split the orders.
Mapping is also done where multiple transformations takes place in order to make them in the format of Emarsys.

Once this data is converted a gatherer is used to gather the data and sent to Predict API in csv format
Sales data can be checked in Emarsys in the predict API in Upload History.

 

 

Get prices in different local currencies.
Currently in Emarsys single tenant there is no option to select multiple currencies at once but sometimes we may need it for localized reporting in smart Insight screens.


In order to achieve this, you can add 2 custom fields one for local currency and another one for Price in local currency. You will also need a mechanism to update the currency conversion in regular interval(defined by users).

 

Once you get these 2 values you can use them to create local currency criterion by combining these 2 filters.

For example your standard currency selection is USD (United States Dollar) and you wanted to segment customers with the criterion people who made a purchase of 100 Euros or more. Here this filtering criterion can be used, we can select 100 from the Local price filter and Euros from Currency filter.

 

Linking orders to both Customer Id and Email.
If your Sales order data contains both Email and Customer Id, Emarsys do not allow you to link your orders using both. Here we will be using a 3rd system which is capable of making calls to Emarsys, this solutioning was done using SAP Customer Platform Integration(CPI)
This solution also involves SAP Customer Platform Integration(CPI) where we make an Http request to Emarsys.



For example here a Http call is made to the system where we have check for email address and it is then sent to CPI where as the data is received it is added to the payload and sent to Emarsys.

entry.title: Business Value of GenAI in Customer Service
entry.content:
Can your business afford to lag while competitors harness business AI to transform their customer service operations? The question is not whether to adopt AI, but how soon can you afford to do so?

With the market size for generative AI in customer service projected to surpass USD 2.9 B by 2032, the urgency is quite undeniable. Gartner predicts that by 2030, AI will manage 40% of customer service engagements. Their Impact of GenAI Survey showed 69% of tech CEOs are keen in using GenAI for customer success/service. Clearly, businesses that fail to start embedding AI now will be struggling to fit a patchwork of AI into their existing solutions later, and risk missing out on significant efficiency gains in the future. Unsure where to put their investment, they will be either a ‘late majority’ or a ‘laggard in the market. 

Let’s talk about what an early adopter organization could potentially do in customer service. By combining generative AI with enterprise service data, early adopters are set to deliver stand-out digital experiences that increase first call resolution rates (FCR) for agents so that they find dot precise answers to customer queries at the first go!

Imagine a service agent, Emily who can now generate answers with a single click. Questions buried in long email threads are highlighted and surface to the top of her feed. AI scavenges the answer content for the agent from past emails, conversations, files etc. then, LLMs provide a natural language answer, grounded in the company's information. Additionally, she can see the sources behind each answer. Simply put, Emily is empowered by an intelligent QA at her disposal to answer customer queries on the go! With the help of AI tools, she can prepare a case overview summary, and collaborate with internal stakeholders to expedite the resolution. After effective collaboration, she can resolve the case on time and update the customer via email, which is auto generated in a concise manner by AI.

When a ticket is created (from, let’s say, social media), the service ticket categorization is automatically done, enabling her to easily route it to the relevant teams or other agents. In case, similar type of incoming tickets are seen, Emily is recommended top 3 similar ticket solutions to check for possible answers. Since she has access to 360 customer profile, she knows latest updates on the customer, be it recent cases or new marketing promotions. When receiving an email from a contact, she we can see a natural language customer profile ‘biography’ that has been generated from the unique customer profile. 

Relevant to Industry? ‘Utilities’ industry, for example, can benefit from AI-powered customer service solutions - from managing service inquiries, billing, move-in/move-out processes, automated support, energy usage optimization etc. As an AI empowered service agent, in a Utilities contact center, Emily, can help customers efficiently with their move-in /move out requests. When she is routed to an incoming call from a customer who is moving to a new home, she can view customers 360 profile details (such as contracts, premise details, point of delivery etc.) Emily quickly gathers additional information (verifies pending outstanding, unbilled units during move-out etc.) and then schedules the service activation. Now, if she receives an email from a customer with a billing dispute on last month’s high consumption, then AI quickly analyzes customer sentiment, retrieves the customer’s billing history/ data to identify the issue – recommends a solution with possible corrections (full or adjustment reversal, corrected meter readings, updated utility rates/ better plans, list of devices that might have spiked consumption etc).

Not only Utilities, but several other industries, such as Retail, Telco, Banking & Financial Services, Healthcare etc. are well-positioned to be early adopters of GenAI in customer service due to their high volume of customer interactions and huge potential for significant efficiency gains. 

Business Value? Early adopters are also keen to measure the business value potential of GenAI in customer service. The two value levers are centered around cost/operational efficiency and NPS / revenue impact.

  1. Cost Savings: A 2023 recent McKinsey study estimated that applying generative AI to customer care functions could increase productivity from 30-45% of current function costs. To quantify such outcomes, first movers consider the impact on top two KPIs: first call resolution rate (FCR) and average handling time, which collectively enhance agent productivity. With AI, if FCR rate can jump by 10-20 percentage points, reaching 75-90%, no more than 10-25% of customers should need to call back regarding the same issue. The business can thus anticipate substantial savings on repeat inbound calls and reduced operating costs. A recent NBER research found out that agents who leveraged AI could handle 13.8% more customer inquiries per hour and time spent handling an issue reduced by 9%. It also reduced agent attrition and requests to speak to a manager by 25%. Now, all this time saved by agents can be allocated to high-value tasks, proactive outreach, strategic process improvements, and enhancing customer relationships. For a typical 2000 FTE contact center, saving even 5-10% of an agent’s time (be it case summarization, ticket categorization or auto email drafts), would equate to saving $3.5M – $7M in FTE costs (based on average $35k salary of an agent). 
  2. NPS and revenue impact: Using LLMs, customer data can be analyzed, rapidly processed, and actioned on. The technology can identify product suggestions, offers tailored to customer preferences, detect patterns and correlations, and focus on improving the experiences of potential promoters while addressing the concerns of potential detractors.

Early Adopter or Early Majority? If your organization hasn't yet become an early adopter, it's time to pinpoint where Generative AI can make the most substantial impact. Start by incrementally integrating AI tools to augment service operations (be it hyper personalization, harnessing intelligence from customer conversations, generating automated summaries or email replies for agents, co-pilots etc.). Invest in enabling service agents to leverage AI effectively and continuously monitor & optimize performance with gradual adoption. As the technology matures, in years to come, your organization should aim to be...

  1. Equipped to set up AI governance or maturity framework.
  2. Figure out how to scale AI across departments.
  3. Able to (incrementally) measure quantifiable business outcomes with AI usage.

Tomorrow’s future showcases fully AI-enabled service organizations that will deliver unmatched personalization, higher productivity, and intelligent customer support experiences – Can your business afford to be left behind?

If you are ready to get started with generative AI to drive business value, check here to know more!

entry.title: Create the Fiori App in a few steps using Tables with SAP ABAP RAP On BTP Platform.
entry.content:
Introduction

In this post, we can create the Fiori app in just a few steps using a table to perform CRUD operations using the ABAP RESTful Application programming model(RAP) on the S/4 HANA cloud platform and Business transaction platform(BTP) using Managed Scenario.

Pre-requisites:

  • S/4 HANA Cloud System
  • Business transaction platform(BTP)
  • Eclipse IDE

In this Blog, I'll explain to you, how we can create the FIORI application using a table without following the manual steps, check it out below.

In our case, all the below objects will be generated automatically.

 

Step 1.

We must create the Database table as per the requirements on the S/4 HANA Cloud System to create the transactional FIORI Application.

Create the DB Table Using Eclipse.

you can see the below, I have created the DB Table to hold the information about Traveling(Travel Booking), you can copy the below table definitions and create your own DB Table.

@EndUserText.label: 'Travel Data'

@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE

@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT

@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A

@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED

define table ztraxxxx_data {

  key client: abap. clnt not null;

  key travel_id         : /dmo/travel_id not null;

  agency_id             : /dmo/agency_id;

  customer_id           : /dmo/customer_id;

  begin_date            : /dmo/begin_date;

  end_date              : /dmo/end_date;

  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'ztraxxxx_data.currency_code'

  booking_fee           : /dmo/booking_fee;

  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'ztraxxxx_data.currency_code'

  total_price           : /dmo/total_price;

  currency_code         : /dmo/currency_code;

  description           : /dmo/description;

  overall_status        : /dmo/overall_status;

  attachment            : /dmo/attachment;

  mime_type             : /dmo/mime_type;

  file_name             : /dmo/filename;

  created_by            : abp_creation_user;

  created_at            : abp_creation_tstmpl;

  local_last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;

  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

Step 2:

Right-click on the table that is already created, and select the option - 'Generate ABAP Repository Objects', as shown in the below image. This option will give you the way to create the FIORI application.

 

Step 3:

In this step, we have 2 options in the ABAP RAP Model,

  • Odata UI Service       --> Select this option to generate the FIORI Elements UI
  • Odata Web API Service

     

Step 4:

Select the Package, where the objects are going to be stored.

Here, you can change the RAP Artifacts names as per your naming convention which are automatically taken care of by RAP Framework, or you can continue with default names.

Step 5:

In this step, you can see what the ABAP Artifacts are generating, you can see here, all the objects that are required to create manually to create the Fiori app, it is generating this automatically.

Click on next to capture the all objects in our TR(Transport Request) and Finish the process.

Kudos...👏

Now you have to create the Transactional FIORI App in a few steps only.

 

Step 6:

Final Step, Select the Service Binding, which is created in Step 6,

It was created the Binding type - Odata V4 - UI, and now just click on 'Publish' to publish the FIORI Application.

 

 

After Publish, It will generate the Service URL, select the Entity Set, and click to Preview to run the Fiori app on the browser.

 

Wow, Now the FIORI Transaction Application is created with Create, Delete, Edit, and Draft Functionality.

Enjoy the FIORI app Development in simple steps by using ABAP RAP on a Cloud Platform.

The Required Artifacts are already generated which will help us to modify the FIORI Application as per our Requirement.

 

I hope this tutorial is helpful to all...

Please feel free to share your suggestions and comments.

Enjoy SAP!!!

entry.title: Create an Issue in SAP Quality Issue Resolution from an Alert in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
The Alert Service in SAP Digital Manufacturing introduces human decision making into the resolution of exceptions happening on the shopfloor. You can now leverage a new powerful integration scenario that lets you react to alerts by creating an issue in SAP Quality Issue Resolution directly from the Manage Alerts app.

Introduction

The Alert Service in SAP Digital Manufacturing aims to notify production operators or supervisors about events or exceptions happening on the shop floor and introduce human decision making when responding to them. Alerts can either be created manually within PODs or automatically by production processes. They contain, as alert properties, the relevant manufacturing execution context such as plant, work center, resource, operation, material, SFC, order, and so on.

 

All alerts appear in the “Manage Alerts” app on the Fiori launchpad of SAP Digital Manufacturing and are managed there. Managing alerts means analyzing and acting upon the alerts and promoting them within their (customizable) status sequence: New >> Acknowledged >> In Process >> Completed.

Certainly, alerts can be acted upon outside the SAP Digital Manufacturing application so that only status changes of alerts are maintained by users processing alerts in the Manage Alerts app until they finally reach the status “Completed”.

However, the Alert Service lets you configure action buttons for the different alert types. Action buttons enable alert processors to directly trigger - from the Manage Alerts app - production processes specifically designed to respond to alerts or send out predefined notification emails to users who can contribute to resolving the alerts.

SAP Digital Manufacturing comes with different alert types predefined by SAP – among them the alert type “Quality Request”, which customers can use when creating alerts to respond to quality events, for example, the discovery of quality issues during the production process. Alternatively, customers can define their own alert types.

Customers who use both solutions, SAP Digital Manufacturing and SAP Quality Issue Resolution (QIR), can leverage a new API made available in QIR to create “issues” in QIR from action buttons in alerts, for example, configured for alerts of type “Quality Request” (and similarly for custom alert types – in our example “Quality Alert”).

Alert properties providing manufacturing execution context information and additional free text entries can be passed on as payload to the API when clicking on the action button. As a result, a new “issue” is created in QIR where it is picked up by a quality engineer who decides whether it needs to be turned into a more formal problem-solving process to resolve the issue in a collaborative and fully documented manner alongside a standard methodology such as “8 Disciplines (8D)” adopted by the discrete industries the customers is part of.

Flow at Runtime

Let’s start by walking through the flow from an end user perspective. The persona who creates the alert on the shop floor is usually a production operator, worker, or a supervisor. If you are using the SAP predefined alert type “Quality Request”, the user triggers the alert from one of your PODs (Production Operator Dashboards) via the “Raise Alerts” POD plugin as shown below. It allows the user  to enter an alert description manually, define an alert processor and recipients for in-app notifications, and choose if a notification email is to be sent as well.

The other option is to create custom alert types. Custom alert types cannot be created from the “Raise Alert” plugin. Instead, customers need to customize their PODs and add an additional button to their PODs and bind a production process to the button that creates the alert. In the screenshot below, the yellow button “Quality Alert” has been added to the POD. When you click this button, an alert of custom type “Quality Alert” is created. Here, the content and recipients of email and in-app notifications sent upon alert creation are configured at design time within the alert type. However, it provides customers with a degree of freedom to define alert properties that best match the purpose. Alert properties can contain manufacturing execution context information or values of sensor tags.

Besides manual creation of alerts, it is also possible to create alerts of either type automatically while running production processes. Alerts can be accessed directly from the email and in-app notifications or via the “Manage Alerts” app on the Fiori launchpad. The recipients in a quality context are usually quality engineers, dispatchers, or supervisors.

New in-app notifications are highlighted with a red circle that appears right next to the bell icon on the top right of the launchpad.

Clicking the bell icon opens the notification bar. Clicking on the message opens the details view of the alert that generated the notification.

Notification emails contain a link at the bottom that also opens the alert details view.

The alert details view contains alert header information, the alert property values providing context information, and buttons to change the alert processor or to change the alert status.In addition, they can contain action buttons, if defined for their respective alert type. In the example below, two action buttons are available. The action button “Notify Quality Engineers” sends a predefined email to a predefined list of recipients. When you click this action button, you have the option to change both the email and the list of recipients.

The other action button “Create Issue” creates an issue calling an API in QIR. A popup showing the required input parameter appears (see below). The first one “plant” is prefilled from the alert property “DME_Plant” whose value was taken from the manufacturing execution context from which the alert was created in the first place. The second parameter “descriptionShort” is a field required in QIR to create an alert. The alert processor can enter free text before clicking OK. He or she can now change the status of the alert to “Completed”.

This is where the flow ends in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Next in the flow is a quality engineer logging on to SAP Quality Issue Resolution (QIR) to check for new issues in the “Manage Issues” app:

He or she can use filters to search for issues. The issue listed below is the one created from the alert:

You can see the new issue with the name “paint peels off from bike frame”. This name, the plant ID “1010”, and plant name “Plant Walldorf” were taken from the payload of the API call. All other fields are left blank. The quality engineer can edit the issue and add missing information and decide whether to create a problem-solving process for the issue or rather to end the processing of the issue after an initial analysis and to set it to complete. In this example, we have provided only the minimum set of required payload parameters to create the issue in QIR via API call from SAP Digital Manufacturing. In the next sections you will learn more about different ways to enrich the payload to provide additional context for the issue.

 

Prerequisites

You require administrator access to the following apps in SAP Digital Manufacturing:

  • Manage Web Servers
  • Manage Service Registry
  • Design Production Processes
  • Manage Alert Types (role Alerts_Admin)

You require a subscription to SAP Quality Issue Resolution and on its launchpad you need access to this app:

  • Manage Issues

You need to ask your tenant administrator to create the following destination to your QIR tenant in BTP Cockpit for your SAP Digital Manufacturing subaccount:

Name: QIR_QIssue_Destination
Type: HTTP
URL:<URL of issues api endpoint from service key JSON>
Proxy Type: Internet
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID: <your Client ID from service key JSON>
Client Secret: <your Client Secret from service key JSON>
Token Service URL Type: Dedicated
Token Service URL: <your URL from in service key JSON>/oauth/token

You can look up the needed values in the service key JSON of your QIR service instance.

 

For more information on how to create a service instance and service key, see the Administration Guide for SAP Quality Issue Resolution.

Overview of Configuration Steps

The configuration for the full scenario can be divided into two parts:

  • Alert generation
  • Alert response via action button

The alert generation part is not covered in this blog. It is covered already in the following documentation:

  • Configuration Guide “Enable DM Alert Service 2405” attached to SAP Note 3435403
  • An updated version of this configuration guide will be published as of release 2408 as a new section in the Operations Guide for SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • Documentation of the DM Alert Service in the SAP Help Portal
  • Application Help for POD Designer explaining how to customize PODs, add buttons and bind production processes to them

This blog focuses on the configuration of the alert response via action button, in our example, creating issues in QIR via action button in alerts.

Since action buttons can be configured either to send predefined emails or trigger production processes, we first need to configure a production process that creates an issue in QIR via API call. For this, we need to create a respective service that does that. Hence, the sequence of configuration steps is as follows:

  1. Create a web server requiring the HTTP destination mentioned in the prerequisites section.
  2. Create a request body and response schemas for the service
  3. Create the service calling the API to create issues in QIR
  4. Create a production process feeding required payload parameters for the API call
  5. Configure an action button for our example custom alert type “Quality Alert”

Create Web Server

In SAP Digital Manufacturing, you need to create a web server that reflects the QIR application.

  1. Open the “Manage Web Servers” app on the Fiori Launchpad and click Create
  2. We have given it the name QIR_QIssue_Webserver; don’t enter a plant
  3. Under Service Details, choose type “Cloud services”
  4. Enter as host URL your URL from the service key JSON as explained in above section
  5. Then, add the destination mentioned in the prerequisites section and save.
  6. Now open the web server “DMC_Cloud”.
  7. In the “Connection” section, click Create and search for the web server “QIR_QIssue_Webserver” you have just created. Select it and then save it.

Create Schemas in Service Registry

You need to create four schemas in the “Manage Service Registry” app. The schemas are attached to this blog as txt file with the respective name. Click on the “Schemas” tab and proceed as follows for each one of the four schemas listed below: Click on the Create button, then click on “Switch to Code” and copy the schema from the respective txt file  into the schema definition frame of the UI and save.

  1. Request body schema QIR_Issue_RequestBody
  2. Subschema to_TextsSchema.
  3. Subschema to_ProductsSchema.
  4. Response schema QIR_Issue_Response.

Now, include the second schema to_TextsSchema as StructureArray to the third schema to_ProductsSchema by opening the latter schema in Edit mode, clicking on “Add”, and placing the cursor in the last highlighted line. Enter “to_Texts” as the name, select “StructureArray” as data type, and select to_TextsSchema from the library as schema. Choose Save.

Finally, add schema to_ProductsSchema as structure to the first schema QIR_Issue_RequestBody. Make sure you select “Structure” as data type this time:

Create Service in Service Registry

Create a new service in the “Manage Service Registry” app, which calls the API endpoint in QIR to create issues and handles the request body and response payloads. Make sure you select the web server created in the section above and enter as endpoint /odata/v4/IssueService/Issues .
Use the same values for Type, Method, Protocol as shown in the screenshot below.

 In the request body section choose “application/json” as content type with data type “Structure” and select the QIR_Issue_RequestBody schema from the library.

 In the response section, add the line as shown in the screenshot below and select the schema “QIR_Issue_Response” from the library.

Design Production Process to Call the Service

The next step is to create a production process, which calls the service you have just created:

  1.  Open the “Design Production Process” app on the launchpad and create a new production process design:

     



  2. Click on the Create button on the canvas. Enter a name for the new production process and select “Cloud” as runtime type.
  3. Click on the three dots “…” highlighted in the screenshot below and activate the toggle “Publish to Service Registry”:

  4. Click on the pencil icon and on “Select Services” in the left pane, and check boxes along the path within the web server “QIR_QIssue_Webserver”. Then click on Save.
  5. You can now draw the production process in the graphical editor of the designer tool. Drag and drop the Start control, the QIR_Issue_Creation service, and the End control from the left pane into the production process canvas. Then connect the Start control with the rectangular service box and the box with the Endcontrol by selecting the start object, clicking on the arrow icon that is shown on the top right and drawing it to the destination object, i.e. the service box and the End control, respectively.

     

Once you’re done, the process should appear as shown in this screenshot:

The following payload fields of the request body of the service are required and can’t be left empty:

  • plant
  • plantName
  • descriptionShort
  • sourceSystemId

Here you have the following options:

  • Create a start parameter for the production process and assign the parameter to the payload field in the right pane of the designer app.
  • Hardcode a value in double quotes, for example, for sourceSystemID enter “Alert in SAP Digital Manufacturing”.
  • Include additional services from the service registry into the production process that perform lookups and pass on looked up values as parameters to the payload field. For example, you can use the service “Reads Plants” to look up the “description” of a plant “1010” and pass it on as a parameter to the payload field “plantName”.

Let’s continue by defining start parameters for the payload fields “plant” and “descriptionShort”:

  1. Select the Start control in the graphical editor, click on “Manage Parameters” in the right pane and add the two parameters “plant” and “descriptionShort” as required parameters of data type “String” and save.
  2. Now, select the rectangular box representing the service in the graphical editor. The right pane will now show the payload fields of its request body. Click on the dropdown icon next to the payload parameters “descriptionShort” and “plant” and select the start parameters you have just created accordingly

You can provide values for start parameters in the following ways:

  • Pass on values from alert properties. This is configured in the “Manage Alert Types” app at design time and will be explained further below. This option is useful for the “Plant” field as most alert types have plant as an alert property.
  • Enter the values manually while managing alerts in the Manage Alert appat runtime when clicking the action button – for example, to enter the “descriptionShort”.

Next, we will provide hard-coded values "Plant Walldorf" and "Alert in SAP Digital Manufacturing" for the required payload parameters “plantName” and “sourceSystemId” respectively. This assumes that you are dealing with one plant only so that you can conveniently hard code it. Note that there is usually no alert property available for it to pass on its value to a start parameter. Simply enter the two values with double quotes in the respective fields in the right pane:

Now, you can click “Save All” and then “Quick Deploy” to test your set up. Click on “Debug” and enter you plant ID and a short description of the quality issue in the respective fields of the pop-up window and click on “Debug”:

This will bring you to the “Monitor Production Processes” screen. On this screen, you can run each individual step in your production process by clicking on the next step button.

The blue circle indicates the next step to execute in the debugger. In the screenshot below, the service has been executed successfully. In the Processing Step Log the responses (or in case of errors, the respective error messages) are displayed. As you can see, a new issue with number 400000000000772 has been created in QIR. The test was successful. You can log on to QIR and search for this issue in there.

Let’s return to the assumption of having just one plant so that the “plantName” field can be conveniently hardcoded. If this assumption is false and you run multiple plants, then you may want to look up the plant name from the plant ID at runtime in the production process. Add the “Reads Plants” service from service registry into the production process as a first step:

  1. In the graphical editor, click on the Edit button to create a new version of the production process design.
  2. On the left pane, click the pencil icon and search for the service “Reads Plants” and expand the “plant APIs” node.
  3. Select the first arrow in the canvas connecting the Start control with the service box and delete the arrow.
  4. Drag and drop the “Reads Plants” service from the left pane into the canvas

  5. Connect the Start control with the new “Reads Plants” box and the box with the “QIR_Issue_Creation” box. While selecting the “Reads Plants” box the right pane shows the payload of the request body of this service. It only has the “plant” field. Click on the dropdown next to it and select the start parameter “plant”.
  6. Now, select the other rectangular box on the canvas to display the payload of the request body of the QIR_Issue_Creation service. Click the dropdown icon next to the payload field “plantName” and select “Service_Read_Plant#httpResponse” line item.

  7. This will open a popup. Select the “description” line and click save:

  8. Now, click “Save All”, then “Quick Deploy” and test with the Debugger as explained above:

     


It looks up the plant description in the “Manage Plants” app and passes on the value to the payload field “plantName”:

You’ll have noticed that many payload fields of the request body of the service QIR_Issue_Creation were left empty. Depending on your scenario and individual process requirements, you can either hard code them in the production process design, have the alert processor enter them manually when clicking on the action button in the Manage Alert apps, or keep them empty and leave it to the quality engineer who picks up the issue in SAP Quality Issue Resolution to populate those fields.

Configure Action Button for Alert Type

Since we have successfully set up the production process that calls the service to create an issue in QIR via API call, we are now ready to bind this production process to an action button for any of our alert types – be it SAP predefined or custom defined:

  1.  Open the “Manage Alert Types” app on the launchpad and choose your alert type – in the example below we have chosen alert type “Quality Alert”:
  2. Enter the details view of the alert type, click Edit, and click on the “Actions” tab. On this tab, click on “Create Action” and select “Production Process”.
  3. Enter a name for the label to appear on the alert detail UI, for example, “Create Issue”, and select the production process “P_QIR_CreateIssue”. In the popup that appears, click on the search icon within the field for “plant” and select the property “DME_Plant” from the alert property set type “DME_Shopfloor”:

    The “descriptionShort” field remains empty. It will be entered at runtime when an alert processor clicks on the action button “Create Issue”. Finally click on Add.

A new action has been created for the selected alert type:

The other action “Notify Quality Engineer” is another type of action. It creates a second action button in the alert detail view with the same label to send an email with predefined text body to predefined recipients (which can both be altered at runtime).

Save the alert type.

This concludes the configuration.

entry.title: Extracting contactperson (table PA0105, subtype KONP) via 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR in RSA3
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, 

 

I'm trying to extract the contactperson of employees via RSA3.

(I have them maintained in table PA0105, subtype KONP)

 

My issue is that for some records it does bring back a value, whereas for others it doesn't.

I can't find any discrepancies between the records other than for some of them the timestamp on PA0000 being slightly different than the one in PA0105, however both being active at a given date - could this really be the cause of missing data?

I'm finding it hard to debug where the tcode RSA3 actually finds the data in S/4 (like does it go directly to the PAxxxx tables) and how it determines if it should bring back a value or not.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud SM: Introducing the Environment Access Management Screen
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community members!

I’m excited to announce the launch of our brand-new Environment Access Management screen in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM). This new feature fulfills our Cloud users’ need for enhanced security in managing access to environments.

With this, you can now control the visibility to your environments by setting them as either “Public” or “Private”. This ensures that only authorized users have access.

It's essential to note that admin users must possess one of the following roles to utilize this new screen:

  • ENVIRONMENTS_ACCESS_ALL
  • ADMIN_USER_ALL
  • ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED

Here's an in-depth overview along with the instructions on how to use it:

Navigating to the Screen

To open the Environment Access Management screen, first choose the Menu option.
Next, navigate to Administration, and then choose Environments Access:

The Environment Access Management screen provides a list of all environments available in the system including the following information:

  • Select (checkbox): Mark the environment as needed
  • Environment Description: Displays details of the environment
  • Environment: Defines the identifier of the environment
  • Version: Defines the version of the environment
  • Access: Indicates the access level (“Public” or “Private”) for each environment
  • Authorized Teams: Contains a list of teams who have authorized access to each environment


Note: The screen follows the existing application standards, such as layout, column options, column filters, and so on.
 

Understanding Access Levels

Access levels determine who can view and perform actions with different environments within an application. These levels can either be public or private.

1. Public: By default, environments are set to “Public”. When an environment is “Public”, all users of the application have access to it.
2. Private: Access to environments is restricted to users who are granted permissions fulfilling one of the following conditions:

  • As an administrator, having any of the following roles:
    -  ENVIRONMENTS_ACCESS_ALL
    -  ADMIN_USER_ALL
    - ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED
  • Being member of a team that has been granted access to particular environment

Access restrictions are enforced across all screens that display an environment list, such as Environments, Process Scheduler (used for job creation), Content Management (used for exporting an environment), and others.

Setting Access Level to Environment

Managing access to various environments is crucial for maintaining security and control.
Here’s a guide on how to set access levels to ensure that only authorized users can view and access designated environments.

1. Select the environment(s) for which you want to set the access level by marking the corresponding checkbox. Note that environments are set to “Public” by default.

2. Choose the Edit access for environment(s) icon in the header section to open the dialog.
The dialog includes the following details:

  • Title: Edit access for environment(s)
  • Description: Adjusting access affects all selected environments
  • Placeholder list of the environments selected by the user
  • Access: Radio buttons allow you to select either “Public” or “Private” option
    When a user selects “Private”, the Authorized Teams multiselect component is shown
  • Buttons: CONFIRM and CANCEL

Dialog: Access (Public)

Dialog: Access (Private)

3. Set Access to “Private” and fill in the Authorized Teams by selecting from the dropdown list.

4. Choose CONFIRM to apply the setting.
The access level for "ABC Environment" has been changed from “Public” to “Private”.
The "ABC-Team" is now assigned to the Authorized Teams field.

Access to Environment when Configured as Private

Let’s explore the outcomes of setting environments to “Private” for the following scenarios:

Scenario 1: Privileged Role Access
Condition:

  • User Role: Users must have one of the following roles:
    -  ENVIRONMENTS_ACCESS_ALL
    -  ADMIN_USER_ALL
    - ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED
  • Access: Environment is set to “Private”
  • Authorized Teams: This field is empty/blank or the user is not a member of any team assigned to this field

Expected Result: The user will be able to view and access all environments (both public and private)

Summary: In this scenario, any of the roles ENVIRONMENTS_ACCESS_ALL, ADMIN_USER_ALL, OR ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED grants the user permissions that override access controls based on access level and authorized teams’ membership. As a result, the user will be able to view all environments within the system.

Sample Screens:
Team Management Screen
The user who will be updating the Environment Access Management screen by changing the Access Level and assigning the Authorized Teams must not be member of the ”AAA Team”

Environment Access Management
For the ”ABC Environment”, access is set to “Private”. The “AAA Team” is assigned as authorized team.
For the “AAA Test Env”, access is also set “Private”, but no teams are authorized.
All other environments are set to “Public”.

As you can see, the user has access to all environments, including "ABC Environment" and "AAA Test Env," as shown on the following screens:

Environments

Process Scheduler when adding a Job


Content Management when Exporting an Environment



Scenario 2: Authorized Team Membership Granting Access
Condition:

  • User Role: Users must not possess any of the following roles:
    -  ENVIRONMENTS_ACCESS_ALL
    -  ADMIN_USER_ALL
    - ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED
  • Access: Environment is set to “Private”
  • Authorized Teams: User is a member of at least one of the teams assigned for this field to access environments

Expected Result: The user can access private environments if he/she belongs to at least one of the authorized teams. Additionally, they will have access to all public environments.

Summary: This scenario demonstrates how access to private environments is controlled based on team membership authorization.

Sample Screens:
Team Management Screen
The user who will be updating the Environment Access Management screen by changing the Access Level and assigning the Authorized Teams must meet the following criteria:
Must be a member of the “ABC-Team”

Must not be a member of the “AAA Team”

Environment Access Management
For the ”ABC Environment”, access is set to “Private”. The ”AAA Team” and ”ABC-Team” are assigned as authorized teams.
For the ”AAA Test Env”, access is also set to “Private”, but no teams are authorized.
For the ”AAA Environment”, access is also set to “Private”, and the ”AAA Team” is assigned as authorized team.
All other environments are set to “Public”.

How can you identify which private environments the user can access based on the given criteria?
For private environments, users can view the ”ABC Environment” only because he/she is a member to at least one of the authorized teams. However, the “AAA Test Env” and ”AAA Environment” will not be visible to the user. Furthermore, the user will have access to all public environments.

Environments



Process Scheduler when adding a Job


Content Management when Exporting an Environment




That’s a wrap for this blog post! I hope you find this overview of the new Environment Access Management screen beneficial.

Stay tuned for the latest updates and insights on the next one. Thank you!

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud: What’s New as of 2024-07-02?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community members!   

Hi Community members, I am thrilled to bring you the latest updates related to SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) for the month of June as my first blog post. 

Now, let's jump straight into the newest features and improvements in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud).    

Note: This blog post contains images. In case the images are too small, kindly click on the image to view with a better resolution.

History Monitor 

The new enhancement in the History Monitor is related to log data when users delete logs from History Monitor. In SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) Standard Model, the Erase Data functionality is enabled for users with SYSTEM_USER_ALL and MODELING_HISTORY. Previously, this allowed users to delete History snapshot without any logs that provide who, when and what is deleted in the application in any monitor screen.  

 With the enhancement, when the user with previously mentions rules delete/erase History logs from the History Monitor, the action is now logged in the Application Monitor under the Run Type H_DELETE specifies who deleted the records and when the deletion take place. 

Writer

The Process Management screen has a feature where the users have an option to lock and unlock data slices in the underlying Model Tables of an Editable Query function used by the Input/Output Activity in the Process Management screen. 

This data locking is not being considered by the Writer function when the Model Table is used as its Output function. With the new enhancement, running the Writer function will be cancelled if there are some data slices locked in the Result Model Table.  

See image below for example logs:  

If you would like to learn more about this data slice lock feature, you can refer to this blog post: What's new as of 2022-12-19  

Environment Access Management Screen 

SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) is introducing a new application called Environment Access Management screen, this allows users to manage Environments visibility by designating them as Public or Private, ensuring that only authorized personnel can view and access the Environments. This will provide more security for the data. 

More details about the Environment Access Management Screen can be found in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) blogpost: Introducing the New Environment Access Management Screen.

I hope that you have found this blogpost interesting and insightful to the new features in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) Standard Model. 

To keep you up to date with our earlier blog posts on the newest developments and features in SAP PaPM Cloud SM, you can check out this tag, which contains a complete archive of related content: PaPMCloudWhatsNew 

Thank you and stay tuned for the future What's New series!

entry.title: Why QR IBAN is automatically prefilled in the invoice
entry.content:
If the Supplier has only a QR IBAN defined in the master data (IBAN with interval 30000-31999 on positions 5-9), then the QR IBAN is automatically prefilled to the incoming invoice during the posting. The accountant must provide only a QR Reference (a unique 27chars reference that is required for a QR invoice).

This ensures that the incoming invoice is posted correctly and the payment will not be rejected later by the payment program due to missing QR data.

If the Supplier has other regular IBANs in the master data in addition to the QR IBAN, the QR IBAN is not filled in automatically and all other invoice types can be posted.
